utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items required in the format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with costs: 1) health_coverage: includes plan name, cost (€200/month), and extra details; cost present, so fully specified. 2) property_coverage: includes plan name, cost (€60/month), coverage limit, ok. 3) travel_coverage: includes two plans with individual costs (€80/month and €50/month) and a stated total (€130/month); covers scope; costs specified. 4) additional_services: lists services but no explicit costs; the format requires 'with costs', so this is not fully specified. 5) total_budget: includes monthly total (€390/month) and annual (€4,680), plus comparison and terms; fully specified as a cost breakdown. Therefore, 4 of 5 items are fully specified with all required details.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists: PrimeCare Premium Health Plan; LifeFirst Property Standard Coverage; Adventure Travel Coverage; and LifeFirst Travel Standard Plan. We need to match each to the closest named option in the ground-truth ratings. For health: 'PrimeCare Premium Health Plan' corresponds to 'PrimeCare Premium Plan' under primary health (physical health), which has a rating of 6. For property: 'LifeFirst Property Standard Coverage' corresponds to 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage' under property_protection -> home_content, which has a rating of 9. For travel: 'Adventure Travel Coverage' corresponds to the travel_enhancement_services item 'Adventure Travel Coverage' with rating 10. The regular travel plan 'LifeFirst Travel Standard Plan' corresponds to 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under mobility_coverage -> travel_insurance, which has a rating of 9.
